items.title: Grieving mother says funeral home lacked care, dignity over daughter's remains

children.content: A First Nations family is calling for changes to the "unregulated" funeral industry after complaints about a loved one's burial went nowhere.

gallery.altText: A woman unsmiling with grey hair.

thumbnail.altText: A woman unsmiling with grey hair.

items.title: Spotlight on Welcome to Country ceremonies in wake of Anzac Day controversy

image.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony

children.content: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

items.title: Strongest storm system in 49 years tears through Perth, southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are left without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, as WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

image.altText: A badly damaged apartment block with its roof torn off and metal debris.

items.title: AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north, tourism operators say

children.content: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

image.altText: A picture of a gorge-like landscape and a waterway on a mobile phone.

items.title: Greens stalwart Bob Brown joins fight against small South West sand mine

children.content: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

gallery.altText: Bob Brown photographed in Hobart, February 2017.
Three men standing in front of a green paddock with some trees. One man is holding a "protect our pits" poster
A road surrounded by forest. A car is moving by quickly.

thumbnail.altText: Bob Brown photographed in Hobart, February 2017.
Three men standing in front of a green paddock with some trees. One man is holding a "protect our pits" poster
A road surrounded by forest. A car is moving by quickly.

items.title: Gas workers strike as negotiations on pay deal stall

children.content: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down

children.content: Fossil fuels, which have powered the global economy for centuries, are under threat. And Australia has become ground zero in this global battle.

items.title: West Aussie Hindley makes history with third Giro podium finish

children.content: West Australian cyclist Jai Hindley roars again in the "pinnacle of cycling", finishing third at the Giro d'Italia, as Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard adds a title in Rome to his victories in France and Spain.

gallery.altText: Jai Hindley shakes hands with Jonas Vingegaard as a firework goes off behind them after the Giro d'Italia.

thumbnail.altText: Jai Hindley shakes hands with Jonas Vingegaard as a firework goes off behind them after the Giro d'Italia.

items.title: Tax deduction red flags on the ATO's radar

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tips this tax time, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

image.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

items.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine as deadly strain of virus spreads

children.content: Nearly 250 people are suspected to have died, and more than 1,100 are infected with the rare strain of Ebola in the DRC and Uganda, though the true spread of the virus is feared to be much wider.

image.altText: Five health care workers wearing PPE, facemasks and eyemasks standing lined up out the front of a home.

items.title: Quiz: Are you smart enough to understand the Scripps Spelling Bee?

children.content: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

image.altText: A teenage boy holds a trophy on his head, next to an event official

items.title: Record number of Australian billionaires hold total wealth of $686b

children.content: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

items.title: Why are Australians still getting lost and dying in the outback?

children.content: Kelly’s son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She’s on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.

gallery.altText: A white car driving in the middle of the bush.

thumbnail.altText: A white car driving in the middle of the bush.

items.title: 'Excited to be back': Serena Williams announces return to pro-tennis at 44

children.content: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

image.altText: Serena Williams clenches her fist during a tennis match at Wimbledon.

items.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand submarines under AUKUS

children.content: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Perth property values rise the most in May as overall housing market stalls

children.content: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything

children.content: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

image.altText: A couples legs lying in bed

items.title: Elected official feels 'heightened risk' amid rising threats of political violence

children.content: As threats against elected officials grow, so too is a question in the mind of many politicians — should the public pay for home security for our elected representatives?

gallery.altText: Security cameras are being installed the ceiling of a house.

thumbnail.altText: Security cameras are being installed the ceiling of a house.

items.title: Meg Lanning gets AI slop treatment in team promo

children.content: The controversial takeover of The Hundred franchise, Manchester Super Giants, continues with a promotional video criticised for seemingly being entirely AI-generated.

image.altText: An AI rendering of Manchester Super Giants batter Meg Lanning.

items.title: Police find 11yo boy who went missing in wild weather in Perth

children.content: An 11-year-old autistic boy, who is non-verbal, has been found after going missing from Perth's western suburbs in wild weather on Saturday afternoon.

image.altText: The back of a boy with a green blanket over him and a man hugging him

items.title: How do you build social connections in a sports mad town if you aren't a sporty person?

children.content: While sport is the lifeblood of some country towns, alternatives abound for those looking to build connections elsewhere.
